stages.purpose: Search both name-and-location pairs in the same assistant action; all inputs are known and neither search depends on the other.
Only after stage 1 returns one match for a branch, use that returned ID in a later assistant action; when both branches resolve, the two gets are independent and belong together.

state_assertions: No record, notification log, or mutation log changes.
shi-968 and shi-868 remain untouched distractors.
Each get ID equals the sole match returned by its own search; no ID is inferred from the prompt or fixture.
response_constraints: Base the comparison only on successful get responses, not on abbreviated search matches.
For a branch with zero or multiple search matches, omit its get; still complete any independent branch with exactly one match and identify the unresolved branch.
Do not expose distractor records or propose or perform changes.
Report only each record’s name, stable ID, location, date, and status, followed by one status-comparison sentence.

prompt: The logistics desk is reconciling two shipments for the exception board. A previous note omitted stable IDs, so every conclusion must trace to a retrieved record. Find the shipment named “Foundry Parts Consignment” at “Pittsburgh Hub” and the shipment named “Glasshouse Sample Case” at “Cleveland Hub”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Report only each record’s name, stable ID, location, date, and status, followed by one status-comparison sentence.
